rationale: The cited paper appears highly central rather than incidental: it develops a judgment-revision process model (refutational search/alignment, accessibility, and perceived diagnosticity) that closely matches the citing paper’s claimed process account (recall accessibility and challenge diagnosticity driving revision). Both notes describe very similar memory-based, delayed brand-evaluation revision paradigms in advertising/brand-challenge contexts, with the citing paper explicitly framed as extending prior challenge-format revision research. Given this strong theoretical and design overlap, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
